student_message: I think I'm ready... but I'm probably going to mess up. Sorry. Is that okay?
final_message:
It is completely okay! Math can be hard sometimes, and it is normal to feel a little nervous. You did something amazing just by being brave and saying you are ready. Making mistakes is a super important part of learning, and we will figure it all out together! 💛 

Let's look at reflection symmetry again. A line of symmetry is just a special fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry, meaning the left and right sides match. Some have a horizontal line, matching the top and bottom. You can even make fun shapes by drawing half of a picture on folded paper, cutting it out, and unfolding it! Letters have them too. The letter $$A$$ has a vertical line, while $$H$$ has both. Would you like to try finding one together?

orchestrator_intent.goal: Reassure the student that mistakes are okay and continue introducing symmetry.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student is fearful and worried about making mistakes. Encouragement is needed to build confidence before continuing the lesson.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
